sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 48-year-old female who had a heart attack 2 weeks ago and underwent a cardiac catheterization with stent placement in her right coronary artery.
She reports being thankful to be alive and struggling with the aftermath of the event.
She has been taking her medications as prescribed and has been advised to take it easy.
She reports a little shortness of breath when walking up and downstairs, but no chest pain since the stent was placed.
Her wrist, where the catheterization was performed, is healing well with no signs of infection.
She is right-handed and has been taking her medications as directed.
She has had a few pizzas, but has been trying to eat well and watch her salt intake.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports a little shortness of breath when walking up and downstairs.
Current medications:
Aspirin 81 mg once a day
Brilinta 90 mg twice a day
Crestor 40 mg once a day
Toqual (presumably ToPro) 50 mg once a day
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 127/60
Physical exam:
The patient's blood pressure is within normal limits.
There is no jugular venous distention, no carotid bruits, and no signs of cardiac tamponade.
A slight 3/6 systolic ejection murmur is heard at the left base.
The lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally.
The right radial artery cardiac cath site is clean, dry, and intact with a palpable radial pulse.
Test Results:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is a 48-year-old female with a history of inferior myocardial infarction and mild mitral regurgitation.
Problem list: Inferior myocardial infarction; Mitral regurgitation; suspected
Plan:
I prescribed Brilinta 90 mg twice a day for at least one year to prevent stent thrombosis.
I advised her to continue taking aspirin 81 mg once a day lifelong.
I prescribed Crestor 40 mg once a day for cholesterol management.
I ordered an echocardiogram in 6 months to assess for any changes in cardiac function.
I advised her to continue with ToPro 50 mg once a day.
I also ordered liver function studies and a lipid panel to be performed at the next visit.
I enrolled her in cardiac rehab for dietary education and counseling.
I advised her to resume swimming with caution and start with short laps.
Follow-up: I want to see the patient again in 6 weeks.
